focus: Build the exhaustive root-resolution call-site inventory, including exact files, lines, callers, and precedence.
noveltyJustification: All 6 findings are new in iteration 1; the inventory converts unverified hints into a cited precedence and caller map and reduces the resolver surface to four behavioral classes.
keyQuestions: Which call sites resolve or enumerate spec roots, what exact precedence does each use, and which writers or readers consume each result?
answeredQuestions: Which call sites resolve or enumerate spec roots, what exact precedence does each use, and which writers or readers consume each result?
ruledOut: Repository-wide literal search as the primary inventory method because generated packets, tests, fixtures, and prose caused truncation and noise
scripts/loaders/data-loader.ts because its roots are input-file security bases rather than packet-root selection
mcp_server/lib/config/spec-doc-paths.ts as a filesystem root selector because production root choice occurs in memory-index-discovery.ts

summary: Shared scripts root enumeration is legacy-first: specs, then .opencode/specs.
generate-context, folder detection, session collection, subfolder search, workflow identity derivation, and directory setup inherit shared legacy-first behavior for unqualified inputs.
MCP spec-document and graph-metadata discovery is canonical-first with legacy fallback only when canonical is absent.
Gate-3 binding enumerates .opencode/specs before specs while accepting either as an explicit boundary.
Direct writers do not share one resolver: tracked spec creation is canonical, untracked creation uses the legacy symlink, and maintenance tools commonly hard-code canonical.
The resolver surface has four behavioral classes, so changing only getSpecsDirectories cannot universalize precedence.
inference: Based on the shared scripts resolver, MCP discovery, Gate-3 root ordering, and direct spec creation writer.

candidate: Repository-wide literal search as primary inventory method
reason: Generated packets, tests, fixtures, schema prose, and documentation overwhelm production root-choice sites and truncate results.
